description: An arc of colors visible in the sky when sunlight refracts through water droplets. 'Loriphi' names the bridge of light—the spectrum revealed when sun meets rain, spanning sky with colors that exist only in the seeing.
sound_symbolism: The word arcs through light. 'Lo' begins with liquid flow, light entering water. Then 'ri' radiates outward, the rays separating. Finally 'phi' /ɸi/ softens into spectrum, the gentle spread of colors.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo loriphi' (the rainbow). Takes the elemental classifier 'themo' when counted. Often singular as each rainbow is a unique phenomenon. Associated with hope, diversity, promise, and the beauty of light.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Rainbows have become symbols of diversity and inclusion—many colors creating one arc of beauty through their differences.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The rainbow's spectrum inspired Art Nouveau color palettes—nature demonstrating that all colors harmonize when light arranges them.
